mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring a specific trigger threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% on 2019-12-01 reflects recent Fed rate cuts from 2.5% in mid-2019
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% on 2019-12-01 is near cyclical lows, providing room for deterioration
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC of UNRATE momentum is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth acceleration simultaneously, it indicates robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The mechanism requires both proxies to move in the same direction within a 1-month window.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive MoM growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1365k units (2019-11) and RSAFS at 527.9B (2019-11) both near cycle highs
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals fail to produce higher mean 20-day returns than single-series signals (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines of more than 1% over a 3-month rolling window signal supply-side weakness or demand destruction that compresses corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 109.677 (2019-11) shows recent strength but vulnerable to demand shocks
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with early signs of supply stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO signal, or Rank IC of 3-month INDPRO momentum is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100k new jobs per month, signaling labor market cooling that precedes broader economic slowdown. This threshold-triggered condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate recession risk and reprrice equity risk premiums upward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152.383M (2019-12) with recent monthly gains averaging 150k+, above trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k, or Rank IC of monthly PAYEMS growth is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% AND inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows year-over-year growth above 2.5%, the Fed is in restrictive policy stance. This regime-conditional interaction signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns as real rates rise and discount rates increase.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% and CPIAUCSL at 258.501 (2019-12) both below trigger thresholds, indicating accommodative stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Joint restrictive signal (FEDFUNDS > 2.5% AND inflation > 2.5%) fails to produce lower mean 60-day returns than accommodative regimes (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19121.112B (2019-07 vintage) reflects solid growth trajectory through mid-2019
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained GDP growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth rate is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or mean 60-day forward returns during periods of above-2.5% GDP growth are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling deterioration in residential investment demand that precedes broader economic weakness. This threshold-triggered condition produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations and increase equity risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1365k units (2019-11) near cycle highs, providing room for meaningful declines
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market vulnerability
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative HOUST signal, or Rank IC of 3-month HOUST momentum is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
